stories.headline: A.J. Brown rides east to New England
stories.read: Well pardner, the Eagles shipped their star receiver to the Patriots, and that shakes the whole camp. Brown's a clear-cut No. 1 again, now catchin' from Drake Maye, who led the league in accuracy last year, so his cut of the throws climbs and a WR1 season's back on the table. The catch? Romeo Doubs and the rest of New England's pass-catchers just turned into dry wells overnight. Go pan that Brown creek before your leaguemates wake up.

stories.headline: Kyler Murray's got the Vikings' reins -- for now
stories.read: Hold up there, the saloon's got Kyler Murray a 90-percent favorite to win Minnesota's job over young J.J. McCarthy, even with O'Connell callin' it an open showdown till late July. That's a gusher for Justin Jefferson, who pairs with a proven gunslinger who can run for points himself. McCarthy, meanwhile, is fool's gold at his draft price till the pecking order's settled. Don't reach for the rookie when the herd ain't even sure he's startin'.

stories.headline: Bucky Irving back on the range at OTAs
stories.read: Good news off the Tampa trail: Bucky Irving practiced for the first time this offseason, lookin' spry after February shoulder surgery, with no danger of missin' Week 1. He's the lead back again, with Gainwell only ridin' the third-down, pass-catchin' route behind him. That's a heap of touches comin' for a fella whose draft price dipped on injury worry. He's shapin' up to be cheap gold in the middle rounds, so stake your claim before the worry lifts.
